summary_one_line: Input must encode a parseable HTTP response with CRLF-terminated headers so httphdrs.head is non-empty at cleanup.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must contain literal HTTP framing bytes (e.g. 'HTTP/' at the response position and CRLF '\r\n' line terminators) so Curl's header parser pushes entries onto data->state.httphdrs.head, making the head pointer non-NULL when Curl_headers_cleanup walks it.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner seeds carry the literal byte sequence 47 48 54 54 50 2f ('HTTP/') at offsets 0x24-0x29 followed by header text terminated by 0d 0a (CRLF); the W-only coverage of Curl_headers_push (12 hits) and namevalue (10 hits) confirms the parser actually populated the headers linked-list on the winner side, so the for-loop initializer e=httphdrs.head sees a non-NULL head.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds have random bytes at offsets 0x06-0x2c (no concentrated values; mostly %-characters, repeated 'e'/'Z', or short truncations); none of them carries 'HTTP/' literally or CRLF in the right positions, so Curl_headers_push and namevalue receive zero invocations on the L side and httphdrs.head stays NULL — the for-loop's first conditional evaluates false and the true branch is never taken.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The curl harness invokes integer/string CMPs against the literal 'HTTP/' (and the CRLF token) while parsing the synthetic response; cmplog's CmpLogObserver captures those operands and I2SRandReplace substitutes them into the input at matching offsets, planting the required literals far faster than naive's blind havoc; vpc inherits the same I2SRandReplace mutator, so it wins over plain value_profile for the same reason.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the for-loop initializer at headers.c:348 inside Curl_headers_cleanup, blocked side T.
Cmplog and vpc resolve every trial; naive and value_profile resolve zero of ten.
Both decisive pairs differ on the I2S technique only (cmplog vs naive, vpc vs value_profile).
Winners take the true branch and execute the loop body lines 349-352; losers reach the for-loop header but the body is dead.
The branch-flip is driven by whether the headers linked-list is populated: W enters the loop ten times with iteration count divergence T=10 F=30 vs L T=0 F=30.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 348  T=10 F=30  T=0 F=30  for(e = data->state.httphdrs.head; e; e = n) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Curl_headers_push (the function that appends to httphdrs.head) and namevalue (the header-line parser) are invoked only on the winner side.
All ten winner seeds carry the literal bytes 'HTTP/' at offsets 0x24-0x29 followed by a space and 'me' text.
Winner seed bytes at offsets 0x20-0x2f spell 'GHTTP/ mK@S' followed by CRLF and a header name, while losers have no such structure.
Loser seeds are short (32-38 bytes for four of five) and contain no 'HTTP/' literal — only random havoc bytes.
Cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of naive's mutator stack; the mutator scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other.
Value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and relies on CMP_MAP gradient feedback only.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 69 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 004b74409cb04016` and `... --seed 006459fd40731a4e`. The first chain has 16 ancestor levels with I2S-floor dash rows at depth 14 and 15; the second has 21 ancestor levels with I2S-floor dash rows at depth 19 and 20 (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only metadata). Both winning seeds therefore inherit from at least one unwrapped I2SRandReplace find. Per the MECHANISM CONTEXT cmplog paragraph, dash rows are exclusive to cmplog/vpc in the current LibAFL build and indicate I2SRandReplace contribution (lower bound — some I2S finds leak into the havoc bucket so this underestimates the true frequency).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a hand-crafted input that omits the literal 'HTTP/' and CRLF at the expected offsets still causes Curl_headers_push to populate httphdrs.head (e.g. because some other parser path on the harness appends a header), then non-NULL head at line 348 is not actually gated on those literals and the I2S-substitution story is wrong.

weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row floor confirms I2SRandReplace contributed somewhere in both winners' ancestor chains, but the dash rows sit 14-20 levels above the leaf — havoc on intermediate ancestors could be what actually flipped the gate, so we cannot prove that the specific 'HTTP/' bytes at 0x24-0x29 were planted by I2S rather than by lucky havoc on an I2S-seeded ancestor.
